tasks.title: Add Missing Instance Isolation Test
tasks.description: Add test_env_instance_isolation test to verify that EnvManager::set() does NOT affect std::env. FR-403.9 reference. This test ensures that EnvManager instance operations are properly isolated from the global std::env namespace.
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: verify EnvManager::set() isolation - test sets variable on EnvManager instance and confirms it's accessible via EnvManager::get() but NOT via std::env::var()
AddUnitTest: verify empty EnvManager instance returns None for unset keys
AddRegressionTest: ensure test_env_instance_isolation passes in isolation
RunTestSuite: execute cargo test -p opencode-core -- env to confirm no regressions
tasks.impl_notes: Add test to #[cfg(test)] mod tests in opencode-rust/crates/core/src/env.rs. Test must verify: 1) env.set() works on instance, 2) env.get() returns correct value, 3) std::env::var() does NOT see the isolated variable. Status: ⏳ Pending.

tasks.title: Review #[allow(dead_code)] on EnvManager struct
tasks.description: Determine why EnvManager is marked #[allow(dead_code)] — either add actual use point or remove suppression. FR-405 reference. Technical debt cleanup for future sprint.
test_criteria: AddUnitTest: grep codebase to find all EnvManager instantiation sites
AddUnitTest: verify each method on EnvManager has at least one call site
RunTestSuite: cargo build to confirm no dead code warnings if suppression is removed
AddRegressionTest: ensure all existing tests pass after changes
tasks.impl_notes: Options: 1) Add use point where EnvManager is used for subprocess env propagation, 2) Remove #[allow(dead_code)] if struct is truly internal-only and suppression is justified, 3) Document why suppression exists if kept. Status: ⏳ Pending. Estimated effort: 15 minutes.
